[IMAGE CLUE]
(Alex delivers the clue from a library at the Harvard Lampoon.) Now a fixture as a late-night TV talk show host, in the 1980s this very tall redhead was a 2-year president of the "Harvard Lampoon"

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Alex holds a framed picture in a library at the Harvard Lampoon.) The style & irreverence of "Harvard Lampoon" had a huge impact in the '70s when alums Doug Kenney & Henry Beard founded "National Lampoon" & Doug co-wrote this very popular college comedy film

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Alex delivers the clue from a library at the Harvard Lampoon.) Lisa Henson was the "Lampoon"'s first female president & she helped her dad Jim write the speech that was given by this Muppet during the Harvard commencement season in 1982

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Alex delivers the clue from a library at the Harvard Lampoon.) Ex-Lampooner Jim Downey not only wrote for "Saturday Night Live" longer than anybody else, he also created this perennial list read by David Letterman

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Alex holds a framed picture in a library at the Harvard Lampoon.) Writers & performers who've gone from the "Lampoon" to "Saturday Night Live" include this young fellow who co-hosts "Weekend Update" with Michael Che

final.clue: According to Herodotus, a messenger was sent 150 miles from Athens to Sparta, just before this 490 B.C. battle
